title: salvia funerea var. typica
intro: Salvia funerea var. typica, also known as the black sage, is a variety of the Salvia funerea plant, which belongs to the mint family and is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Salvia funerea var. typica include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and support the immune system. Salvia funerea var. typica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
